note: LIVE-data manifest for lean-6module-v1.1 freshness gate (INC-001 re-verification). Every LIVE field carries >=2 independent sources fetched at as_of; verify_freshness.py cross-checks these against an independent Yahoo re-fetch and the decision_card value. FILED data (10-K/10-Q line items) is NOT here -- it rides the dated-source claim_ledger discipline. Price $298.01 (2026-06-18 last close <= as_of) re-verified CORRECT vs Yahoo repo fetcher and stockanalysis.com; this dossier does NOT share the NVDA single-source bug (price is +49.8% off the 52wk low, -6.1% off the 52wk high -- not hugging either extreme).

live_fields.note: Yahoo 52wk low 198.96 (card primary); stockanalysis 195.07. ~2% disagreement on the trailing-low print, immaterial to T1/T2 since price 298.01 is +49.8% off either value.

live_fields.value: No formal next-quarter numeric revenue guidance re-verified here; anchor quarter is reported actuals (Q2 FY26, ended 2026-03-28): revenue $111.2B/+17%, Services $31.0B all-time high, Greater China $20.5B/+28%, GM 49.3%, EPS $2.01/+22%, new $100B buyback authorization, dividend raised to $0.27. Forward stance is qualitative (installed-base annuity + Services mix); no stale numeric guide.

Google search-remedy antitrust outcome re-verified 2026-06: final remedy order (April 2026) imposes a default-deal ban + choice screens effective June 2026; Apple/Samsung/Mozilla must implement choice screens. Court did NOT bar Google from paying distribution partners (judge: cutting off payments would impose substantial downstream harm) and rejected Chrome/Android divestiture; DOJ + 35 states are appealing for tougher remedies. Net: Google can still pay Apple but contracts shorten to ~1-yr terms -> TAC volatility, not an outright zeroing. Confirms the dossier's O1 framing (~$20B/yr high-margin Services flow at risk, magnitude/timing OPEN) -- a margin haircut, not structural breakage. Not stale.
